title: Quick-DNA™ FFPE Kit Protocol
author: Zymo Research
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.giybufw

steps.title: Remove (trim) excess paraffin wax from...
steps.instruction: Remove (trim) excess paraffin wax from sample and transfer the sample to a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. Note: Up to 25 mg tissue from a paraffin block or up to four (4) tissue sections (≤20 μm thick) with a 2 total surface area ~20 cm . It is recommended to use start with 1-2 sections. Note: If using fresh/frozen tissue specimens proceed directly with Proteinase K Digestion & DNA Isolation.

steps.title: Add 400 μl of Deparaffinization Solution to the sample
steps.instruction: Add 400 μl of Deparaffinization Solution to the sample. Incubate at 55°C for 1 minute. Vortex briefly. 00:01:00 Note: Xylene may also be used for deparaffinization. See the Appendix for more instructions.

steps.title: Remove Deparaffinization Solution from the sample...
steps.instruction: Remove Deparaffinization Solution from the sample and proceed to next section.

steps.title: To the deparaffinized tissue sample (≤...
steps.instruction: To the deparaffinized tissue sample (≤ 25 mg) in a microcentrifuge tube, add the following mixture: %s %s Note: If the tissue sample is too large for the digestion volume, scale up the digestion to 200 μl while keeping the amount of Proteinase K the same. Double the reagent volumes indicated in Step 8 & 9 of the DNA Purification section.

steps.title: Digest 1-4 hours or overnight at 55 °C
steps.instruction: Digest 1-4 hours or overnight at 55 °C. %s %s Rapid Digestion Standard Digestion Incubate at 55°C for 1-4 hours Incubate at 55°C overnight (12-16 hrs) Note: The Rapid Digestion is recommended for processing slide tissue sections. The Standard Digestion ensures maximum yields of DNA from tough-to-lyse (collagen-rich, fibrous, etc.) or large tissue samples.

steps.title: Transfer the digestion to 94°C and incubate for 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Transfer the digestion to 94°C and incubate for 20 minutes. 00:20:00

steps.title: Once done, add 5 μl of...
steps.instruction: Once done, add 5 μl of RNase A , mix, and incubate an additional 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Add 350 μl of Genomic Lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl of Genomic Lysis Buffer to the tube and mix thoroughly by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 135 μl of isopropanol 1...
steps.instruction: Add 135 μl of isopropanol 1 (user supplied) to the sample and mix thoroughly. 1 ssDNA will also be purified if present in the sample upon the addition of isopropanol. This procedure will isolate total DNA > 50 bp. To isolate only DNA > 500 bp, skip this step. FFPE DNA may be highly degraded and DNA >500 bp may not be present in sample.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 12,000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 12,000 x g for 1 minute to remove insoluble debris. 00:01:00 This procedure will isolate total DNA > 50 bp. To isolate only DNA > 500 bp, skip this step.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a Zymo-Spin™...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a Zymo-Spin™ IIC Column 2 in a Collection Tube . The maximum loading volume for the Zymo-Spin™ Column is ~700 μl.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. 00:01:00

steps.title: Add 400 μl of Genomic DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μl of Genomic DNA Wash 1 to the spin column in a new Collection Tube .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. Discard the flow-through. 00:01:00

steps.title: Add 700 μl of Genomic DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 700 μl of Genomic DNA Wash 2 to the spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 12,000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 12,000 x g for 1 minute. Discard the flow-through. 00:01:00

steps.title: Add 200 μl of Genomic DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of Genomic DNA Wash 2 to the spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ≥ 12,000 x g for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ≥ 12,000 x g for 1 minute. 00:01:00

steps.title: Transfer the Zymo-Spin™ IIC Column to a clean microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the Zymo-Spin™ IIC Column to a clean microcentrifuge tube. Add ≥ 50 μl DNA Elution Buffer 3 or water (add ≥100 μl if sampling 25 mg tissue) to the spin column. 3 Elution of DNA from the column is dependent on pH and temperature. If water is used, ensure the pH is >6.0. Also, the total yield may be improved by eluting the DNA with Elution Buffer or water pre-equilibrated to 60-70° C or by performing and pooling sequential elutions.

steps.title: Incubate 2-5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate 2-5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Centrifuge at top speed for 30...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at top speed for 30 seconds to elute the DNA. 00:00:30

steps.title: The eluted DNA can be used...
steps.instruction: The eluted DNA can be used immediately for molecular based applications or stored ≤-20°C for future use.
